channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: US says Iran’s new supreme leader alive and ‘increasingly engaging’
item.description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio says Iran’s Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei is increasingly engaging in negotiations.

item.title: Israeli settlers torch Palestinian homes in village near Ramallah
item.description: Khirbet Abu Falah has been frequently targeted by settler attacks, with over 30 incidents reported since late March.

item.title: What Trump ‘telling off’ means for Netanyahu
item.description: US President Donald Trump reportedly lashed out at Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu during a phone call.

item.title: Ukraine’s Kostyuk blasts Russia stars in French Open outburst over war
item.description: Ukraine's Marta Kostyuk faces Russia's Mirra Andreeva in semifinal after beating Elina Svitolina at Roland Garros.

item.title: Norway supports ethics complaint against FIFA’s Infantino on World Cup eve
item.description: NFF president Lise Klaveness has called on FIFA to scrap the FIFA Peace Prize award to protect its political neutrality.

item.title: Iran’s supreme leader appears more active as talks continue: US’s Rubio
item.description: Tehran has agreed to discuss previously off-limits aspects of its nuclear file, US State Secretary tells lawmakers.

item.title: French PM to fast-track security bill after PSG football violence and riots
item.description: More than 200 people were arrested in France following PSG's Champions League win, which followed semifinal disorder.

item.title: UK police bodycam video shows wrongful arrest of dying teen
item.description: Newly-released bodycam video shows the moment UK police wrongfully arrested 18-year-old Henry Nowak as he was dying.

item.title: Scene of damaged hospital after Israeli strike in southern Lebanon
item.description: Al Jazeera’s Obaida Hitto is at the scene of Jabal Amel Hospital, which was ‘significantly damaged’ in Israeli attacks.

item.title: US targets Brazil with new tariffs over trade practices
item.description: The US plans new 25 percent tariffs on Brazilian imports, citing issues like deforestation and digital trade practices.

item.title: US university sells dead bodies to navy for Israeli military training
item.description: The AJ+ documentary series Direct From investigates connections between body donations and Israeli military programmes.

item.title: Canada seeks USMCA renewal for 16 years, sector tariff discussions
item.description: Trade discussions between the two countries have been frozen since late last year.

item.title: UN tells world to brace for extreme weather as El Nino looms
item.description: Potential impacts, including floods, drought and heatwaves, could 'hit even harder' this year, UN chief warns.

item.title: China opens its markets to African exports. Who benefits?
item.description: China's tariff-free access for many African products has renewed hopes of industrial growth. But trade remains uneven.

item.title: Intisar Shanib becomes first woman to head football club in Libya
item.description: Shanib has been appointed as the president of Darnes Sports Club, a prominent football club in Libya's Derna city.

item.title: Could Russia hit northern Europe if it gained control of Arctic’s Bear Gap?
item.description: Norwegian defence minister warns Russia could pose a grave risk and must not be allowed to control the corridor.

item.title: The Horn of Africa needs reconciliation, not new borders
item.description: Recognising Somaliland would not deliver stability. It would entrench division, in Somalia and well beyond it.

item.title: Could Trump’s government be influencing Canada’s separatist movement?
item.description: Alberta will vote in a referendum over whether to separate from Canada, but how is the US President involved?

item.title: Is the Taliban-Russia MoU good for Afghanistan?
item.description: The recently signed agreement is unlikely to advance the country's long-term national interests.

item.title: Albanians protest Kushner plan for Sazan Island luxury resort
item.description: Hundreds have rallied in Albania against plans by a Jared Kushner-linked investment firm to develop Sazan Island.

item.title: ‘Encroached territory’: Why India-Nepal border dispute is heating up again
item.description: Balendra Shah's recent comments about Nepal encroaching on Indian territory have revived a 200-year-old border dispute.

item.title: ‘Trapped’: Gaza patients flown to Iraq stuck in administrative limbo
item.description: Palestinian patients stripped of their documents are confined inside a Baghdad medical centre.

item.title: Nearly 5,000 staff, students urge Belgium’s universities to cut Israel ties
item.description: A letter representing more than 10 percent of the Belgian professoriate calls for end to all collaborations with Israel.

item.title: Israeli attacks on Lebanon continue after Trump saying they’d stop
item.description: Israeli air strikes on southern Lebanon have continued, a day after US President said the ‘shooting’ would stop

item.title: World Cup delay for Switzerland’s Embolo while US reviews travel document
item.description: Embolo is Switzerland's first-choice forward, with 24 goals in 86 internationals, but could not join the squad.
